notes.title: God works all things for good
notes.body:
Paul's climax of assurance. The **"good"** is not comfort but being
*conformed to the image of the Son* (v29). Then the golden chain:
foreknew → predestined → called → justified → glorified — every link the
same people, none lost.

Compare [[Genesis 50:20]]: what others meant for evil, God meant for good.

notes.title: No condemnation
notes.body:
*Katakrima* (condemnation) — a judicial verdict, not a feeling. The chapter
opens where chapter 7's struggle ends: the verdict is already delivered for
those *in Christ Jesus*.

notes.title: Romans 8 — the mountain-top chapter
notes.body:
Often called the greatest chapter in the Bible. Movement: **no condemnation**
(1-4) → **life in the Spirit** (5-17) → **groaning creation & hope** (18-27) →
**unbreakable love** (28-39). The Spirit is named ~19× after being nearly
absent in ch.7.

notes.title: Romans — background
notes.body:
Written ~AD 57 from Corinth to a church Paul had not yet visited, ahead of a
hoped-for trip to Spain. His most systematic statement of the gospel: the
righteousness of God revealed for Jew and Gentile alike, received by faith.

notes.title: For God so loved the world
notes.body:
The gospel in one sentence. *Houtos* = **"in this way"** loved — pointing back
to the lifted-up serpent (v14-15): love shown by the Son lifted up on the
cross. "Whosoever" throws the door wide.

notes.title: Nicodemus & the new birth
notes.body:
A Pharisee comes *by night*. Jesus reframes religion as **rebirth**, not
reform — born of water and Spirit. The wind/Spirit wordplay (*pneuma*) drives
the whole dialogue.

notes.title: The LORD is my shepherd
notes.body:
David moves from speaking *about* God ("He") to speaking *to* God ("you") at
the valley (v4) — intimacy deepens precisely in the dark. Shepherd imagery
picked up by Jesus in [[John 10:11]].

notes.title: Topic: Assurance of salvation
notes.body:
A thread to preach or teach: God's keeping, not our gripping. Anchor in
[[Romans 8:38-39]], [[John 10:28]], and [[Philippians 1:6]] — begun, carried,
completed by God.

notes.title: Topic: The Holy Spirit
notes.body:
Regeneration ([[John 3:5]]), indwelling and intercession ([[Romans 8:26-27]]),
sealing ([[Ephesians 1:13-14]]). The same Spirit who hovered at creation.
